lines.text: The descent begins. What was buried here was buried for a reason.
The first floor — where curiosity and dread are still balanced. That balance will not last.
From here, it is still possible to return. The dungeon does not say this to encourage retreat — only to mark the moment when it ceases to be true.
Deeper. The air tastes of old ink and older regret.
The second level. The descent is committed now — whatever that means for what remains of you.
The first floor was the vestibule. This is the dungeon proper — where the knowledge the surface world forgot was placed, deliberately, out of reach.
The bottom. Where knowledge goes to become something else entirely.
Very few reach the third floor. Of those, fewer still with enough left to matter. The dungeon has been filtering.
It did not die. Guardians do not die. It returned to the knowledge it was made from.
The chamber still vibrates. Boss encounters leave scars on the architecture.
The final level. What you have carried this far — every correct answer, every mistake, every relic — will be weighed against what waits below.
You emerged. The dungeon will not forget this. Neither will you.
Your knowledge joins the archive. It will be used — recycled into questions for the next seeker. Nothing is lost down here. Only reclassified.
